output:
**(B) No — an infinite group cannot be isomorphic to a finite group because no bijection exists between them.**

An isomorphism requires a bijection (one-to-one and onto map) that preserves the group operation. Since ℤ is infinite and ℤ/7ℤ is finite, no such bijection can exist. They're both cyclic, but "cyclic" is a family of groups, not a single structure — ℤ and ℤ/nℤ are non-isomorphic members of that family.
